title: Cooks
description: Direct and may participate in the preparation, seasoning, and cooking of salads, soups, fish, meats, vegetables, desserts, or other foods. May plan and price menu items, order supplies, and keep records and accounts. Directly supervise and coordinate activities of workers engaged in preparing and serving food. Prepare and cook large quantities of food for institutions, such as schools, hospitals, or cafeterias. (Based on 8 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Record production or operational data on specified forms.
Coordinate planning, budgeting, or purchasing for all food operations within establishments such as clubs, hotels, or restaurant chains.
Arrange for equipment purchases or repairs and check equipment to ensure it meets health and safety regulations.
Meet with sales representatives to negotiate prices or order supplies, and inspect supplies to ensure conformance to established standards.
Demonstrate new cooking techniques or equipment to staff, and instruct cooks or other workers in food preparation.
Meet with customers to discuss menus for special occasions, such as weddings, parties, or banquets; and collaborate with personnel to plan menus.
Recruit and hire kitchen staff, and train new employees in food preparation and safety procedures.
Determine production schedules and staff requirements to ensure timely delivery of services, and estimate necessary food supplies.
Prepare and cook foods of all types, either on a regular basis or for special guests, and cook foodstuffs according to diet restrictions or portion numbers.
Analyze recipes to assign prices to menu items based on costs and forecast staff and supply requirements.
Plan, direct, or supervise food preparation and cooking activities in facilities such as restaurants or hospitals.
Check the quality of raw or cooked food products, and ensure food is stored and cooked at safe temperatures.
Monitor sanitation practices and ensure hygiene standards are followed in food preparation areas.
Stock, organize, and clean kitchens and cooking utensils; and supervise cleaning of food preparation areas and equipment.
Portion, arrange, and garnish food for serving, and serve meals and snacks to patrons or guests.

detailed_work_activities.title: Prepare foods for cooking or serving.
Train food preparation or food service personnel.
Manage food service operations or parts of operations.
Check quality of foods or supplies.
Coordinate timing of food production activities.
Inspect facilities, equipment or supplies to ensure conformance to standards.
Coordinate activities of food service staff.
Estimate supplies, ingredients, or staff requirements for food preparation activities.
Create new recipes or food presentations.
Maintain food, beverage, or equipment inventories.
Clean food preparation areas, facilities, or equipment.
Monitor food services operations to ensure procedures are followed.
Store supplies or goods in kitchens or storage areas.
Evaluate quality of food ingredients or prepared foods.
Adjust temperature controls of ovens or other heating equipment.
Apply protective or decorative finishes to workpieces or products.
Operate cooking, baking, or other food preparation equipment.
Communicate with customers to resolve complaints or ensure satisfaction.
Monitor activities of individuals to ensure safety or compliance with rules.
Monitor equipment operation to ensure proper functioning.
Measure ingredients or substances to be used in production processes.
Shape clay or dough to create products.
Move equipment, supplies or food to required locations.
Assist chefs or caterers with food or drink preparation.
Evaluate quality of materials or products.
Present food or beverage information or menus to customers.
